headline: The Bull Case For Agilent Technologies (A) Could Change Following Exclusive FDA Approval Of Its KEYTRUDA Test
summary: Agilent Technologies has recently received U.S. FDA approval for its PD-L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx assay as the only companion diagnostic to identify esophageal and gastroesophageal junction carcinoma patients eligible for treatment with KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab). This exclusive companion diagnostic status strengthens Agilent’s role in precision oncology by directly linking its assay to treatment decisions for a difficult-to-treat cancer segment. Next, we’ll examine how becoming the sole...
